questions.question: Individuals who currently have minority or majority ownership stakes in two or more independent businesses that are either new, purchased and/or inherited can be classified as ______ entrepreneur.
questions.explanation: Portfolio means collection. A portfolio entrepreneur owns multiple businesses at the same time.

questions.question: The predisposition to have particular beliefs, attitude and behavior represented is ______.
questions.explanation: Personality is what makes you have certain beliefs, attitudes, and ways of acting.

questions.question: The gap in the market that represents the possibility of doing something new, different and better and so the chance to create value is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: An opportunity is a gap in the market where you can do something new and create value for customers.

questions.question: Individuals who believe that what happens to them is a result of fate, chance, or forces beyond their control is said to have ______.
questions.explanation: External locus means you think outside forces like luck or fate control what happens to you.

questions.question: Environmental factors such as the population, interest rate and inflation that influence business activities can be classified under ______.
questions.explanation: Population, interest rates, and inflation are all economic factors that affect businesses.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a component of a competitive market?
options: The taste and preferences of consumers
The intensity of competition within the industry
The ease with which firms can enter the industry
questions.explanation: Taste and preferences are part of socio-culture, not the competitive market. Competitive market looks at rivals, substitutes, and entry barriers.

questions.question: The following are roles of small businesses to economic development except ______.
options: They contribute to forward and backward linkages
They make efficient use of scarce factors
They make efficient use of scarce factors
questions.explanation: Small businesses do not abandon local technology. They actually use and improve it.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Role of Small Businesses in Development

questions.question: The following are advantages of starting your own business except ______.
questions.explanation: New businesses face barriers, they do not automatically overcome them. The other options are real advantages.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - Advantages of Starting a Business

questions.question: The required number of participants in a focus group discussion ranges from ______.
questions.explanation: A focus group should have between 8 and 14 people for good discussion and control.

questions.question: Which phase of a new venture lasts until the venture is terminated or no longer controlled by the entrepreneur?
questions.explanation: The post start-up phase covers everything after launch, including growth and the end of the business.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3 - Phases of Venture Creation

questions.question: A business agreement in which a company grants others the right and license to sell a product or service is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Franchising is when a company gives someone else the right to sell its products.

questions.question: Business plan can be triggered by all the following events except ______.
questions.explanation: You write a business plan for big things like starting a business or buying one, not for small daily choices.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1 - When Business Plans Are Needed

questions.question: The following are options available to the entrepreneur to be involved in existing business except ______.
questions.explanation: BIMBO is a special case. The normal options are buy-in, buy-out, and outright purchase.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Options for Small Business Buyers

questions.question: A business is said to have a goodwill if the business starts with no backlog of problems.
questions.explanation: False. Starting with no problems is called a clean sheet. Goodwill means good reputation with customers.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - Clean Sheet and Goodwill

questions.question: Advantages of buying existing business include the following except ______. I. Insider knowledge II. Existing track record III. Not all my own work IV. Buying market share V. Potential ill will
questions.explanation: Not all my own work and potential ill will are disadvantages, not advantages. The others are real advantages.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Advantages of Buying Existing Business

questions.question: Which of the following is not a source of new business idea?
questions.explanation: Brainstorming is a method to come up with ideas, not a source. Sources are places like customers and existing businesses.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Sources of Business Ideas

questions.question: Trade name franchising provides the franchisee with a complete business format.
questions.explanation: False. Trade name franchising only gives you the right to use the brand name. Pure franchising gives the complete format.

questions.question: Which among the following are not advantages of franchising to the franchisee? I. Management training and support II. Franchise fees and profit sharing III. Less freedom IV. Knowledge of the market V. Standardized quality
questions.explanation: Franchise fees and profit sharing are costs you pay. Market knowledge is not a direct advantage to the franchisee.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Advantages and Disadvantages of Franchising

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the control elements in a business plan?
questions.explanation: Disbursement just means paying money out. It is not listed as a control element like inventory or marketing control.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 6 - Control Measures in Business Plans

questions.question: According to Hisrich and Peters, business plans may fail due to ______. I. Unreasonable goals II. Goals not measurable III. Lack of commitment IV. No experience
questions.explanation: All four cause business plan failure: goals that are not reasonable, goals you cannot measure, not being committed, and having no experience.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 3 - Causes of Business Plan Failure

questions.question: What differentiates opportunist entrepreneurs from traditional entrepreneurs?
options: Opportunist entrepreneurs focus solely on profit, while traditional entrepreneurs prioritize social impact.
Opportunist entrepreneurs are quick to adapt to changing circumstances, whereas traditional entrepreneurs follow a fixed business model.
Opportunist entrepreneurs exclusively pursue large-scale ventures, while traditional entrepreneurs engage in small businesses.
Opportunist entrepreneurs always work independently, while traditional entrepreneurs prefer partnerships.
questions.explanation: Opportunist entrepreneurs are flexible and quick to change. Traditional entrepreneurs stick to a fixed plan.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Types of Entrepreneurs (Opportunist Entrepreneur)

questions.question: What term is used to describe individuals who are in the early stages of preparing to start a new business?
questions.explanation: Nascent means just beginning. Nascent entrepreneurs are getting ready to start a business but have not launched yet.

questions.question: What distinguishes novice entrepreneurs from portfolio entrepreneurs?
questions.explanation: Novice entrepreneurs have no prior experience. Portfolio entrepreneurs own multiple businesses, so they have experience.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Novice vs Portfolio Entrepreneurs

questions.question: Miss Eunice Agyei's political exit and movement into business represents which sociological perspective?
questions.explanation: Between things means people who leave one role (like politics) and move into business because they are in transition.

questions.question: An individual who believes they have the skills, resources, and opportunity to run a business experiences ______.
questions.explanation: Perception of feasibility means you believe you can actually do it because you have the skills, resources, and chance.

questions.question: Kwesi trained as a carpenter and started his own shop with apprentices. Kwesi is ______.
questions.explanation: Craft entrepreneurs use their hands-on skills like carpentry to start a business.

questions.question: Which involves showing a preliminary product/service to customers to gauge interest?
questions.explanation: Concept testing means showing an early version or description of your product to see if customers like it.

questions.question: Which section of a business plan includes competitors and target customers?
questions.explanation: The market analysis section looks at your customers, competitors, and the industry trends.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a primary audience for a business plan?
questions.explanation: Competitors are not your audience. You write a business plan for yourself, your team, your investors, and your lenders.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1 - Audience of a Business Plan

questions.question: Which section of a Full Business Plan contains organizational structure analysis?
questions.explanation: The operations plan explains your management structure, staff, and how the business will run day to day.

questions.question: Who is a social entrepreneur?
questions.explanation: A social entrepreneur uses business to solve social problems like poverty or pollution, not just to make profit.

questions.question: What is the importance of entrepreneurship in economic development?
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurship helps the economy by creating jobs and bringing new ideas and products.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Entrepreneurship and Economic Development

questions.question: Which trait is associated with successful entrepreneurs?
options: Embrace challenges and take calculated risks
questions.explanation: Successful entrepreneurs face challenges head on and take smart, calculated risks.

questions.question: Which is an example of an entrepreneur?
questions.explanation: An entrepreneur starts and owns their own business. An employee works for someone else.

questions.question: An entrepreneur is known for what portfolio?
questions.explanation: A portfolio entrepreneur manages several different businesses all at the same time.

questions.question: Primary goal of brainstorming?
questions.explanation: The goal of brainstorming is to come up with as many different ideas as possible, no matter how wild.

questions.question: What source involves buying rights to operate a proven business model?
questions.explanation: Franchising lets you buy the rights to use a business model that already works.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Franchising as Idea Source

questions.question: Personality represents predisposition to beliefs, attitudes and behaviours.
questions.explanation: True. Personality is what makes you tend to have certain beliefs, attitudes, and ways of acting.

questions.question: Entrepreneurs with internal locus of control believe that ______.
questions.explanation: Internal locus means you believe your own actions control your success, so outside factors do not matter as much.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Internal Locus of Control

questions.question: Why conduct a feasibility analysis before committing to a business idea?
questions.explanation: Feasibility analysis helps you see the risks and problems before you spend too much time and money.
